title: ligularia tongkyukensis
intro: Ligularia tongkyukensis, also known as Korean ligularia, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, particularly in China and Korea. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. In traditional herbal preparations, Ligularia tongkyukensis is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
